notes: This file records staged bring-up semantics conventions that are consumed by the Sail formal model and referenced by the manual.
These conventions are normative for the strict-v0.3 bring-up profile unless overridden by a later revision.

regdst_writeback.description: Destination selector semantics for RegDst-like fields that can target GPRs or ClockHands pushes.

no_output.behavior: No architectural output is produced (silent drop). No exception is raised.

convert_type_fields.description: 5-bit SrcType/DstType coding for the Convert instruction family (v.*cvt*). Reserved encodings are illegal and must not be generated by software.

intent.note: Convert instructions perform numeric conversions, not bit reinterpretations. Float-specific rounding/exception behavior is defined by FSU/CSTATE rules.

trap_encoding.description: Bring-up normative trap cause encoding conventions used by this repo (Sail/QEMU/LLVM alignment).

regid10.description: RegId10 operand subfields and width/type conventions used by v.* instruction families (strict bring-up).

float_operands.note: Additional float formats (e.g. bf16, e4m3, e5m2) are encoded via SrcType/DstType on convert-class instructions; RegId10 width is only a width tag.

srcrtype.order: Apply SrcRType first, then apply any explicit shift (e.g. <<<shamt or <<shamt in addressing).
srcrtype.word_ops: For *W (32-bit) operations, apply modifiers at 32-bit width, compute a 32-bit result, then sign-extend to 64-bit.

restricted_forms.description: For forms whose assembly syntax only allows {.sw,.uw} (e.g. CMP.{EQ,NE,LT,GE,LTU,GEU} and SETC.{EQ,NE,LT,GE,LTU,GEU}), SrcRType=11 is treated as 00 (no modifier) in strict v0.3.

csel.description: CSEL encodes an optional .neg on SrcR; other SrcRType values are treated as 00.

setc_immediates.description: Immediate-compare conventions for SETC.*I forms with an encoded shamt field.
setc_immediates.encoding: SETC.*I encodes simm/uimm plus a 5-bit shamt (bits[11:7] in 32-bit forms, analogous position in HL forms).
setc_immediates.assembly: Assembly syntax elides shamt (prints a single immediate). Assembler/tooling may choose shamt to represent larger aligned constants when encodable.

pc_relative.base: current instruction PC (scalar blocks) or current instruction TPC (vec bodies); the instruction description says PC/TPC.

addtpc.note: ADDTPC uses a signed immediate (addtpc simm).

setret.note: SETRET uses an unsigned immediate (setret uimm).

word_writeback.description: For *W variants, compute in 32-bit domain then sign-extend result to 64-bit.

scalar.note: Scalar MUL/MULU return the low 64 bits of the full product.

word_writeback.description: For *W variants, compute in 32-bit domain then sign-extend result to 64-bit.

hl_two_dest.description: HL.MUL/HL.MULU return the full 128-bit product split across two destinations.

hl_madd.description: HL.MADD/HL.MADDW return a 128-bit accumulator split across two destinations.

block_legality.description: Legality conventions for which instruction families may appear as payload in coupled scalar blocks vs engine bodies.

bru_control_transfer.description: BRU control-transfer instructions (B.* / J / JR / etc.) are not legal payload in coupled scalar blocks; they are vec-engine scalar-lane-only.

on_scalar_block_execute.name: ILLEGAL_INST
on_scalar_block_execute.note: Cause/arg payload TBD; strict profile at minimum traps with TRAPNUM=4.

pc_relative.jr_srczero: The encoded SrcZero field is ignored (treated as 0) in strict v0.3.
pc_relative.jr_alignment: No forced alignment; odd target is permitted (subsequent fetch may fault).

tpc_misalignment_fault.name: BLOCK_TRAP
tpc_misalignment_fault.note: Use E_BLOCK(EC_BFETCH) for non-MMU body fetch faults (alignment).

tpc_invalid_target_fault.name: BLOCK_TRAP
tpc_invalid_target_fault.note: Vec-engine BRU targets that are outside the vec-body fetchable region (or otherwise not fetchable) are treated as body-fetch faults (not CFI).

body_range.note: No explicit architectural [BodyTPC, BodyEnd) range is defined for vec bodies in strict v0.3; fetchability is operational (if fetch fails => EC_BFETCH).

predicate_register.name: p
predicate_register.note: The predicate domain is vec-engine-defined; any architectural mirroring through BARG/EBARG is profile-defined and should not be assumed by scalar-only components.

register_domain.description: Vec engine scalar-lane BRU reads operands from the scalar-lane GPR file (not ClockHands queues).

contract_summary: Scope: vec-engine scalar-lane only (illegal in coupled scalar blocks => TRAPNUM=4 ILLEGAL_INST).
PC-relative scaling: offset is halfword-scaled (target = base + (sext(offset) << 1)).
JR: target = SrcL + (sext(simm12) << 1); SrcZero field ignored.
Predicate: B.Z/B.NZ test vec predicate register p (all-zero vs non-zero).
Faults: misaligned/unfetchable targets => E_BLOCK(EC_BFETCH), BI=1, TRAPARG0=faulting VA.
Body range: implicit/operational (no explicit architectural [BodyTPC,BodyEnd) boundary).
Operand registers: read scalar-lane GPR domain (not ClockHands).
vec_engine_scalar_lane.note: When executed in the vec engine scalar lane, BRU control-transfer updates the body-local TPC (not architectural global PC).

bitfields.description: Bitfield operations (BXS/BXU/CLZ/CTZ/BCNT/BIC/BIS/REV) parameter mapping and wrap-around semantics.
bitfields.wrap_around: Bit ranges are defined on a 64-bit ring. For i=0..N-1, field[i] = SrcL[(M + i) mod 64]. Equivalent: field = ROR64(SrcL, M) & mask_low(N).

alias.ROR: Alias of BXU with Nminus1=63 (N=64): ROR64(SrcL, M).

rev.description: REV performs byte-order reversal on the selected bitfield.
rev.operation: field = bitfield_wrap(SrcL, M, N); if (N % 8) != 0 then result=0 else reverse byte order of low N bits; high bits are zero.
rev.invalid_n: If N is not a multiple of 8, the result is 0 (silent).

prefetch.description: Prefetch / cache hint instructions (PRF/PRFI and HL variants).
prefetch.note: Prefetch provides a performance hint only; it must not architecturally fault in strict v0.3.

floating_point_compare.description: Scalar floating-point compare conventions (FEQ/FLT/FGE and S-variants).
floating_point_compare.s_variants: modeled as aliases (no FP exception signaling modeled in staged Sail)

minmax.nan_behavior: maxNum/minNum (one NaN => other; both NaN => canonical qNaN)

signed_zero.fmax: when both inputs are zeros, return +0
signed_zero.fmin: when both inputs are zeros, return -0 iff any input is -0

fixup_blocks.description: Unmanaged fixup block routing rules (strict v0.3).

unmanaged_fixup_block.definition: BSTART.SYS FALL<, fixup_label (SYS block with explicit fixup_label)

exception_routing.when: synchronous exception occurs while executing inside an unmanaged fixup block
exception_routing.fault_visibility: precise to instruction: all prior instructions commit; faulting instruction has no architectural side effects

assert.cause_note: TRAPNO.CAUSE is populated; for ASSERT_FAIL it is fixed to 0.

masking.when_disabled: ASSERT is treated as NOP (no trap, no trap-envelope update)
masking.scope: ASSERT_FAIL only (other synchronous exceptions are unaffected)
